title: Houstonia nigricans
intro: Houstonia nigricans, also known as the Black-eyed Susan or Yellow Houstonia, is a small, yellow-flowering plant in the coffee family that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Houstonia nigricans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used in various herbal remedies, including salves and ointments for skin issues and fever-reducing tonics.
taxonomy: Houstonia nigricans is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is commonly known as the black houstonia or black twinflower. The plant is native to North America, specifically in the southeastern United States. Houstonia nigricans is a low-growing, perennial herb that produces small, white to purple flowers. It is classified as a species within the genus Houstonia, which includes several other species of twinflowers.
